report.executive_summary:
MEMORANDUM TO FILE – TITLE COMMITMENT ANALYSIS AND CLOSING READINESS ASSESSMENT

RE: Title Commitment No. 01-26006219-01T
Property: 675 Bunker Hill Road, Columbia, MS 39429-7832
Marion County, Mississippi | Parcel No. 074-04-000-04-005
Proposed Buyer: Kyla B. Fortenberry | Lender: Society Mortgage
Commitment Date: January 16, 2026 | Readiness Score: 67/100

CRITICAL ISSUE – TRANSACTION CANNOT CLOSE IN CURRENT STATE

This transaction presents a BLOCKING DEFECT that must be resolved immediately before closing can proceed. Requirement 4 has not been satisfied: the recorded warranty deed (Instrument #20260174, recorded 01/13/2026) names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees, NOT the proposed buyer Kyla B. Fortenberry. This represents a complete failure to convey title to the actual purchaser and creates an insurmountable title defect that will prevent issuance of the owner's policy.

Additionally, a material discrepancy exists between the vested owner (Garner Tree Farm, LLC) and the listed seller (Terri G. Crawley), which must be documented and explained through proper LLC authorization documentation per Requirement 6.

A secondary concern involves duplicate and overlapping exceptions for mineral reservations (Exceptions 1 and 8) that require consolidation and clarification in the final policy language.

Once the deed is corrected to name Kyla B. Fortenberry as grantee and all LLC compliance documentation is submitted, the remaining requirements are routine and should clear expeditiously. The property has no tax delinquencies, and standard easements are properly noted as exceptions.

report.requirements:
TITLE COMMITMENT REQUIREMENTS STATUS

REQUIREMENT 1: Vested Owner vs. Seller Discrepancy
Status: OPEN – REQUIRES DOCUMENTATION
Description: Vested Owner is Garner Tree Farm, LLC; Seller is listed as Terri G. Crawley. Discrepancy must be explained and documented.

Analysis: The commitment shows Garner Tree Farm, LLC as the vested owner holding legal title in Marion County records. However, the transaction lists Terri G. Crawley as the seller. While Terri Garner Crawley is identified as a member of Garner Tree Farm, LLC, the individual seller capacity versus LLC ownership must be clarified. This discrepancy suggests either: (a) the LLC is the proper seller and individual capacity is incorrect, or (b) the LLC has authorized Terri G. Crawley to act on its behalf.

Required Action: Obtain written explanation from seller/LLC regarding the authority structure. This likely requires submission of LLC documentation per Requirement 6.

---

REQUIREMENT 2: Verify Open Deed of Trust
Status: SATISFIED – CONDITIONAL
Description: No open deed of trust found in Marion County records. Verify by inquiry of escrow personnel and/or proposed insured.

Analysis: Title search reveals no open deed of trust encumbering the property in Marion County records. This requirement is satisfied pending final verification from escrow and lender confirmation that no other liens exist.

Required Action: Obtain written confirmation from escrow agent and Society Mortgage that no other liens or encumbrances exist on the property.

---

REQUIREMENT 3: Bankruptcy Search
Status: SATISFIED
Description: No bankruptcy proceedings found for Garner Tree Farm, LLC, Terri G. Crawley, and Kyla B. Fortenberry.

Analysis: Bankruptcy searches have been completed for all parties with no adverse findings. This requirement is satisfied.

Required Action: None – requirement cleared.

---

REQUIREMENT 4: Record Deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC to Kyla B. Fortenberry
Status: NOT SATISFIED – CRITICAL BLOCKING ISSUE
Description: Record satisfactory executed Deed from Garner Tree Farm, LLC, grantor(s) to Kyla B. Fortenberry, grantee(s), conveying the caption premises in Marion County records.

CRITICAL DEFECT: The recorded warranty deed (Instrument #20260174, recorded 01/13/2026) conveys the property FROM Garner Tree Farm, LLC TO Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson. This is NOT the required conveyance to Kyla B. Fortenberry. This represents a complete failure to satisfy Requirement 4 and creates a title defect that will prevent issuance of the owner's policy to the proposed buyer.

Legal Analysis: Under Mississippi law, a deed must name the correct grantee to effectively convey title. A deed naming incorrect grantees does not convey title to the intended purchaser and creates a defect in the chain of title. The current recorded deed creates a situation where:
1. Garner Tree Farm, LLC has conveyed title to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson
2. Kyla B. Fortenberry has no legal interest in the property
3. The owner's policy cannot be issued to Kyla B. Fortenberry
4. The transaction cannot close

Required Action: IMMEDIATE – A corrected warranty deed must be prepared, executed, and recorded naming Kyla B. Fortenberry as the sole grantee. The deed must be executed by Garner Tree Farm, LLC (through authorized representatives) and must convey the same property described in the commitment. The incorrectly recorded deed (Instrument #20260174) may require a corrective deed or quitclaim deed from Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson to clear title.

---

REQUIREMENT 5: Record Deed of Trust
Status: OPEN – ROUTINE
Description: Record satisfactory executed Deed of Trust from Kyla B. Fortenberry as Trustor, in favor of Society Mortgage, as Beneficiary, securing an original principal balance of $80,808.00.

Analysis: This requirement is routine and will be satisfied at closing once Requirement 4 is corrected. The deed of trust must be properly executed and recorded in Marion County records.

Required Action: Prepare and execute deed of trust in favor of Society Mortgage. This requirement will be satisfied at closing once title is properly conveyed to Kyla B. Fortenberry.

---

REQUIREMENT 6: LLC Documentation and Compliance
Status: OPEN – REQUIRES SUBMISSION
Description: Submit: (a) Copy of operating agreement and amendments or certification of none; (b) Evidence LLC is in good standing; (c) Resolution signed by all members authorizing transaction; (d) Other requirements following review of organizational documents.

Analysis: Garner Tree Farm, LLC is a Mississippi limited liability company with four identified members: Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, and Karen Garner Delancey. Before the LLC can execute a corrected deed to Kyla B. Fortenberry, the underwriter must verify that the transaction has been properly authorized by all members and that the LLC is in good standing with the Mississippi Secretary of State.

Required Documentation:
1. Certified copy of the LLC's Operating Agreement and any amendments
2. Certificate of Good Standing from Mississippi Secretary of State (dated within 30 days of closing)
3. Member Resolution signed by all four members authorizing the sale and conveyance to Kyla B. Fortenberry
4. Certification that the LLC has authority to convey the property and that no member consent requirements have been violated
5. Any other organizational documents required by the underwriter upon review

Required Action: Obtain and submit all LLC documentation to Old Republic National Title Insurance Company for review and approval. This documentation is essential to verify that the LLC has proper authority to execute the corrected deed.
